skill.name: when-analyzing-performance-use-performance-analysis
skill.description: Guides Claude Flow performance analysis with baseline measurement, profiling, bottleneck diagnosis, optimization, and validation steps.
skill.summary: Analyze Claude Flow swarm performance and turn profiling data into validated optimization recommendations.
skill.author: DNYoussef

security_audit.summary: The static external command findings are true positives because the skill documents many runnable shell commands for Claude Flow profiling, benchmarking, and optimization. No evidence found of malicious intent, credential exfiltration, prompt injection, or hidden executable payloads; the weak-crypto and system-reconnaissance findings are false positives or low-context documentation matches.

medium_findings.title: Runnable External Performance Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill provides shell command examples that run Claude Flow through npx, collect metrics, profile agents, write reports, and archive performance data. This is legitimate for the stated purpose, but it can execute local tooling and affect the workspace when followed by an agent.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit markdown examples, and their purpose matches the performance-analysis skill. Risk remains because an agent could execute them and alter local files or run downloaded npx code.

medium_findings.title: Automatic Optimization Commands Can Modify Runtime State
medium_findings.description: The optimization phase includes commands that apply recommendations, rebalance agents, optimize memory, and train neural patterns. These actions may change swarm configuration or runtime state and should require user approval before execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The affected commands are documented directly and use action verbs such as optimize, rebalance, configure, and train. They are not malicious, but they have operational side effects.

medium_findings.title: Shell Substitution and Interpolation in Examples
medium_findings.description: The skill includes shell substitutions using date, jq, bc, and variable interpolation. These examples are understandable, but agents should not paste them into untrusted shells without reviewing input files and generated variable values.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The substitutions are visible and limited to timestamps, JSON parsing, arithmetic checks, and archive naming. Confidence is high for the pattern, but medium for exploitability because no untrusted network source is shown.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Match Is False Positive
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged a weak cryptographic algorithm at the skill description line. The line only describes performance analysis and contains no hashing, encryption, credential handling, or cryptographic implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is visible metadata text and has no cryptographic operation. This is a very high confidence false positive.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Static Match Is Contextual Profiling
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged line 161 as reconnaissance. In context, it passes an agent identifier to a Claude Flow profiling command as part of documented performance measurement.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding lines show profiling of listed Claude Flow agents, not host reconnaissance or stealth discovery. It remains low risk because profiling can reveal local operational details.

dangerous_patterns.title: npx Execution of Alpha Package Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: Many examples run npx claude-flow@alpha. If the package is not already installed or pinned by the environment, npx may execute code resolved from the package registry.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The npx pattern is repeated and executable. The risk is supply-chain and local execution exposure, not evidence of malicious code in the skill itself.

dangerous_patterns.title: Auto-Apply Optimization Flag
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow includes automatic optimization flags that can apply performance changes without a detailed confirmation step in the documented script.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and related to the skill goal. They are risky because automatic changes can affect a live swarm or workspace.

content.value_statement: Claude Flow swarms can slow down without clear evidence of the cause. This skill guides baseline measurement, profiling, bottleneck analysis, optimization, and validation.
actual_capabilities: Defines a five-phase workflow for baseline, profiling, analysis, optimization, and validation.
Lists Claude Flow commands for collecting swarm, agent, memory, network, and benchmark metrics.
Shows how to classify performance issues by severity and likely impact.
Provides validation targets for throughput, latency, utilization variance, error rate, and overhead.
Includes quick start and complete workflow examples for repeated performance analysis.
limitations: Requires Claude Flow and related command-line tools to be installed and trusted.
Does not include a custom parser, profiler, dashboard, or executable implementation.
Optimization results depend on the target swarm, workload, and available metrics.
Commands that modify swarm state should be reviewed before execution.

use_cases.title: Diagnose Slow Swarm Execution
use_cases.description: Measure baseline throughput and latency, profile the swarm, and identify bottlenecks before changing configuration.

use_cases.title: Validate Optimization Work
use_cases.description: Compare pre-change and post-change metrics against defined targets for throughput, latency, and error rate.

use_cases.title: Standardize Performance Reviews
use_cases.description: Use the documented phases to make performance analysis repeatable across Claude Flow workflows and teams.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Baseline Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to plan a baseline performance review for my Claude Flow swarm. List the metrics to collect and the expected outputs.

prompt_templates.title: Find Likely Bottlenecks
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze these performance symptoms with this skill: high latency, uneven agent load, and intermittent errors. Recommend profiling steps and issue categories.

prompt_templates.title: Create an Optimization Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to turn my benchmark and profiling results into a prioritized optimization plan. Separate safe changes from changes needing approval.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Validation Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design a validation report that compares baseline and optimized results against throughput, latency, utilization, and error-rate targets.

output_examples.input: A swarm has high latency and uneven agent utilization after a workflow change.
output: Baseline current throughput, latency, resource utilization, and error rate.
Profile swarm execution, agent response time, memory usage, and network latency.
Classify likely issues as coordination overhead, load imbalance, or resource pressure.
Recommend incremental changes and validate each result against the baseline.

output_examples.input: A team needs proof that a performance change improved results.
output: Run the same benchmark before and after the change.
Compare throughput, latency, utilization variance, and error rate.
Confirm whether the improvement targets were met.
Document regressions, unchanged metrics, and recommended next actions.

best_practices: Establish a baseline before applying any optimization.
Review every command that changes swarm configuration or runtime state.
Validate improvements with repeated measurements and clear success targets.
anti_patterns: Running automatic optimization commands on a live swarm without approval.
Comparing results from different workloads, durations, or benchmark settings.
Treating a single profiling run as proof of a permanent performance issue.

faq.question: What does this skill analyze?
faq.answer: It analyzes Claude Flow swarm performance through baseline metrics, profiling, bottleneck detection, optimization planning, and validation.

faq.question: Does the skill run commands by itself?
faq.answer: No. It documents commands and workflows that an agent or user may choose to run with approval.

faq.question: Why is there a security warning?
faq.answer: The skill includes many external command examples. Some commands can profile systems or change swarm configuration.

faq.question: Can it optimize any application?
faq.answer: No. It is written for Claude Flow swarms and workflows, although the measurement process is broadly useful.

faq.question: What outputs should I expect?
faq.answer: Expected outputs include baseline metrics, profiles, bottleneck reports, recommendations, comparisons, and final performance summaries.

faq.question: How should I use it safely?
faq.answer: Run read-only measurement steps first, inspect generated recommendations, and approve state-changing commands separately.
